title: Web Design Perth: Fixed Prices, Perth Hours
meta_title: Web Design Perth | Fixed-Price Websites from $750
meta_description: Web design for Perth businesses with fixed, GST-inclusive pricing: $750 Starter or $1,500 Business, no lock-in contracts. Remote, working 9 AM to 9 PM AWST.
intro: OptiTide builds and rebuilds websites for Australian businesses, working to Perth time. Fixed prices, GST included, no lock-in contract, and hours that run to 9 PM AWST.
body_html:
Working with someone on Perth time

OptiTide works to AWST, the same clock as Perth and the rest of WA. Send a question at 3 PM on a Tuesday and it lands in the middle of our working day. Our hours are Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM, and the late finish is deliberate: the working day runs four hours past a normal five o'clock knock-off, so feedback you send in the evening can land inside our hours rather than after them.

To be straight about it, we are not in Perth. The business is registered in Victoria, the work is done remotely, and there is no office to drop into, in Perth or anywhere else. We work with clients right across Australia on that basis. What you get instead of a shopfront is a client portal, where you can follow the state of your project, view and pay invoices, and message us without hunting back through an email chain.

The business is a sole trader: Michael James Long trading as OptiTide, ABN 38 163 865 712, Australian owned and operated. There is more about that on the about page.

What to judge a website by

A web design project is usually one of two things: a first proper website, or a rebuild of something that has aged badly. Either way it helps to be clear about what you are judging the result against, because "looks nice" is not a standard you can hold anyone to. These are the questions worth asking of any site, whoever builds it.

  • Does it load quickly, including on a phone on mobile data?
  • Does it say what you do, where you do it and who you do it for, without the visitor having to scroll and guess?
  • Is the next step obvious, whether that is ringing you, filling in a form or buying something?
  • Is it structured so a search engine can read it and work out what each page is about?
  • Can it grow, so adding a service page in a year does not mean starting again?

Page count, features and the content you need to supply are all worth settling before any work starts. Scope creep in either direction is worse for you than it is for us, so it is better agreed up front than discovered halfway through.

What web design in Perth costs

Prices are published rather than worked out per enquiry. Everything below is in Australian dollars, includes GST, and comes with no lock-in contract. Because GST is inside the figure rather than added to it, the price you agree to is the price you pay.

Starter, $750 one-off

The entry tier, for a business that needs a credible, working presence online: who you are, what you offer, and how to reach you. Suits sole traders and newer businesses that want to stop apologising for their website.

Business, $1,500 one-off

The next tier up. Suits a business with several services or service areas to cover, or one where the website is expected to do more day to day than simply introduce you.

Custom, quoted

Anything that does not fit either fixed tier is quoted individually, rather than squeezed into a price that was never meant for it. Full detail on all three sits on the web design service page.

How the process runs

There is more detail on how we work, but the short version is this.

  • Enquiry and quote. You tell us what the business does and what the site needs to achieve, and we come back on which tier fits. The two fixed tiers are already priced; the custom tier is quoted.
  • What you supply. The parts only you can provide: your logo if you have one, photos, any brand colours you already use, and access to your domain and existing site.
  • Design and build. You see the site take shape and send feedback through the portal, so the record of what was asked for sits in one place instead of scattered across an email chain.
  • Review and launch. Final checks, then the site goes live on your domain.
  • Invoicing. Invoices sit in your portal and can be paid by PayID or bank transfer, Skrill, PayPal or Payoneer.

How long it takes

Two things set the pace, and only one of them is the building. The first is how ready your content is: text, photos, and access to your domain and any existing site. The second is how quickly feedback comes back on each round. A business that has its copy and images sorted moves fast. One that is still deciding what its services should be called does not, and no agency can fix that from the outside.

Ask for a timeline when you enquire and you will get one based on your actual scope. If you want to move things along before you even get in touch, write down your services in the words your customers use for them, and gather any photos of your own work. Both are things nobody else can do for you.

After the site is live

A website is not finished at launch, it just stops being a project. If you want it looked after, hosting is $25 a month unmanaged or $50 a month managed. If you want the site to be found, SEO runs at $750, $1,500 or $2,500 a month across three tiers, and social media marketing is $250 a month. All of those prices include GST, none of it is compulsory, and none of it is locked in, so you can start or stop as it suits you. You can compare the lot on the services page, and we write about this sort of thing on the blog.

Getting a quote

Send through what you have: your current site if there is one, what you sell, and roughly what the new site needs to do. You will get a straight answer about which tier fits, including when the answer is that the cheaper one is enough. Get in touch and we will take it from there.

faqs.q: How much does web design in Perth cost?
faqs.a: OptiTide publishes two fixed one-off prices: Starter at $750 and Business at $1,500, both in Australian dollars and both GST inclusive. Anything that does not fit either tier is quoted individually. There are no lock-in contracts, and you will know which tier you are on and what it costs before any work begins.

No. The business is registered in Victoria and the work is done remotely, so there is no shopfront or office to visit, in Perth or anywhere else. What is true is the clock: we work to AWST, Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM, so a Perth or WA client is dealing with someone on their own time rather than someone who has already knocked off. Day to day you deal with us through your client portal or by email, which is also how we work with clients elsewhere in Australia.

faqs.q: How long does it take to build a website?
faqs.a: It depends more on your content and your feedback than on the build itself. If your text, photos and domain access are ready and you review each round promptly, the project moves quickly. Ask for a timeline when you enquire and you will get one based on your actual scope. Our working day runs to 9 PM AWST, so evening feedback can still arrive inside our hours rather than after them.

faqs.q: Do you also handle hosting, SEO and social media after launch?
faqs.a: Yes, and all of it is optional. Hosting is $25 a month unmanaged or $50 a month managed. SEO is $750, $1,500 or $2,500 a month across three tiers, and social media marketing is $250 a month. All prices are in Australian dollars and include GST, and none of them are lock-in contracts, so you can add or drop them as you need.

faqs.q: How do I pay, and when?
faqs.a: Invoices appear in your client portal, where you can view and pay them, track your project and message us. Accepted payment methods are PayID or bank transfer, Skrill, PayPal and Payoneer. All prices quoted are GST inclusive, so the figure you agree to is the figure you pay.
